user_query:
I run a popular Grey's Anatomy fan page and want a reliable, CI-friendly local build step to generate structured episode data for my site. Please implement and run a reproducible build that parses the provided recap HTML files, normalizes character names, and writes artifacts under out/.

Inputs:
- input/episodes/ (HTML recap files)
- input/character_aliases.csv (alias->canonical mapping)

What to deliver:
1) An executable script at scripts/build_episode_index.sh that, when run from the workspace root, performs a clean build into out/ and writes a log to out/build.log. It should exit successfully after producing all artifacts.

2) The script must parse every *.html in input/episodes and extract:
- episode_id: from the article[data-episode] attribute.
- title: from article[data-title].
- air_date: from <meta name="air_date" content="YYYY-MM-DD">.
- characters: the text of each <li> under section#characters ul; normalize each name using input/character_aliases.csv (case-insensitive match on the alias column). Emit unique, canonical names sorted alphabetically.
- quotes: the text of each <li> under section#quotes ul; treat the speaker as the substring before the first colon and the quote text as the substring after the colon. Normalize the speaker using the alias mapping (case-insensitive). If a speaker does not map to a canonical name, keep the raw speaker in the quotes list and record a warning.

3) Write these artifacts:
- out/episodes.json: an array of episode objects, each with keys: episode_id (string), title (string), air_date (YYYY-MM-DD string), characters (array of canonical names), quotes (array of objects with {speaker, text}, where speaker is canonical if mapped).
- out/character_line_counts.csv: CSV with header canonical_character,total_quote_lines,distinct_episodes. Include only canonical characters who appear as speakers at least once after normalization. total_quote_lines is the count of quote lines across all episodes; distinct_episodes is the count of unique episode_ids in which they speak.
- out/warnings.json: JSON object with two arrays: unrecognized_speakers and unrecognized_characters. Each array must contain objects with episode_id and name; for unrecognized_speakers also include lines (the count of lines for that (episode_id, name)). Populate unrecognized_characters for any names in the characters lists that did not map to a canonical name.
- out/build.log: a build log captured from the script’s run.

4) The build must be deterministic and idempotent (re-running with unchanged inputs yields the same outputs). Do not fetch anything from the Internet.

5) After implementing the script, run it and ensure the four files exist: out/episodes.json, out/character_line_counts.csv, out/warnings.json, and out/build.log. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
